name: Vitamin D3
rationale: Near-universal deficiency in RAD patients. Immune/bone/mood. Modulates adipose inflammation.
notes: Pair with K2 MK-7 100 mcg to route calcium correctly

name: Vitamin K2 (MK-7)
rationale: Directs calcium to bones away from arteries. Mandatory companion for long-term D3.
notes: Import via iHerb; usually combined with D3

name: Selenium
rationale: Thyroid function + antioxidant (glutathione peroxidase). Deficiency documented in ~47% of RAD patients. Cho/Herbst retrospective case: selenium + butcher's broom with physical therapy → 70-79% leg volume reduction (n=1). 2025 scoping review cites conservative 45-60 mcg range.
notes: Brazil nuts rare/expensive in Nepal; tablets typically import. Upper tolerable intake is 400 mcg/day - do not exceed long-term

name: Vitamin C
rationale: One of only two supplements rated "Suggested" by 2025 scoping review (with DHA/EPA). Collagen synthesis + anti-inflammatory. Cannataro 2021 protocol used 1 g/day in two doses.
food_equivalent: Citrus / guava / amla / bell peppers / kiwi
notes: Widely stocked locally (both ascorbic acid and food-based)

name: Diosmin + Hesperidin
rationale: Citrus bioflavonoid. Venous/lymphatic tonic; anti-inflammatory; reduces capillary fragility and edema.
food_equivalent: Small amounts in citrus peel (not therapeutic)
notes: Daflon (Servier India) stocked across South Asia for hemorrhoids/varicose veins. 1-2 tablets/day standard. Consult doc if anticoagulant or bleeding disorder.

name: Butcher's Broom
dose: 300 mg extract 2x daily (Cho/Herbst case used 1 g/day)
rationale: Vasoconstrictor of venous capillaries. Primary evidence: single retrospective case (Cho/Herbst) where it was paired with selenium and physical therapy.
notes: Not typically stocked in Nepal; import via iHerb

name: Rutin
rationale: Flavonoid that strengthens capillary walls; reduces bruising/vascular fragility.
food_equivalent: Goji berries + bee pollen + buckwheat + capers + asparagus
notes: Usually food-covered via goji and bee pollen; pill only if capillary fragility persists

name: Vitamin B12 (methylcobalamin)
rationale: Nerve function + energy + RBC. Often low in RAD; risk higher if red meat restricted.
food_equivalent: Animal products only (meat/fish/eggs/dairy); fermented foods do NOT provide reliable B12

name: Magnesium glycinate
rationale: Muscle + sleep + insulin sensitivity + vascular tone. Best-absorbed calm form. 2025 scoping review classifies as "evaluate" tier for lipedema — strong evidence for cramps/sleep generally, not lipedema specifically.
notes: Import typical; glycinate form rarely stocked locally

name: Magnesium citrate
rationale: Acceptable alternative to glycinate; also mildly laxative.
notes: More commonly stocked locally than glycinate

name: Marine hydrolyzed collagen peptides
notes: Already in protocol; watch for artificial sweeteners (sucralose concern)

name: Bee pollen granules
rationale: Rutin + quercetin concentrated; lymphatic vessel integrity.

name: Pea-brown rice protein powder
rationale: Hypoallergenic protein alternative to whey (which is insulinogenic / IGF-1 concern).

name: Himalayan pink salt in morning water
rationale: Electrolyte management; trace minerals; supports hydration especially on low-carb

name: Omega-3 fish oil (EPA+DHA)
dose: 1-2 g combined EPA+DHA daily (Cannataro 2021 used 3 g)
rationale: Anti-inflammatory. One of only two supplements rated "Suggested" by 2025 scoping review (with vitamin C). Use when fish intake <2x/week.

name: Multi-strain probiotic
rationale: Gut health + immune. Often already covered via kefir/yoghurt in protocol.
notes: Local options exist but quality varies; consider import for specific strains

name: Zinc picolinate
rationale: Immune + skin + wound healing. Monitor copper balance long-term.

name: CoQ10 (ubiquinol)
rationale: Mitochondrial energy; consider if fatigue / post-exertional malaise.
